articles.title: Ny forening skal styrke Innovation District Copenhagen

sentences.text: En ny forening skal sikre, at Innovation District Copenhagen kan udvikle sig som et internationalt kraftcenter for forskning og iværksætteri.
sentences.translation: A new association will ensure that Innovation District Copenhagen can develop as an international powerhouse for research and entrepreneurship.
sentences.grammarNote: 'skal sikre, at' uses 'skal' to express intended function or purpose ('is meant to ensure'), not obligation â the same pattern you saw with 'skal levere' in the energy article.

sentences.text: Formålet er at samle forskere, læger, iværksættere og virksomheder i ét tæt økosystem omkring Københavns Universitet og Rigshospitalet.
sentences.translation: The purpose is to gather researchers, doctors, entrepreneurs, and companies in one close ecosystem around the University of Copenhagen and Rigshospitalet.
sentences.grammarNote: 'FormÃ¥let er at + infinitive' â when the subject complement describes an action with the same implied subject as 'formÃ¥let', Danish uses 'at' + infinitive directly rather than a full at-clause with its own subject.

sentences.text: Området skal især blive et internationalt centrum inden for life science og kvanteteknologi.
sentences.translation: The area is meant to especially become an international center within life science and quantum technology.

words.grammar: always two words in this sense

sentences.grammarNote: 'inden for' (within) is always written as two words in this meaning â a small but high-frequency spelling distinction worth fixing early.

sentences.text: Initiativet bygger videre på en politisk aftale, som staten, Københavns Kommune og universitetet indgik tidligere.
sentences.translation: The initiative builds further on a political agreement that the state, the City of Copenhagen, and the university entered into earlier.

words.grammar: infinitive: indgå (same verb seen in the research-agreement article)

sentences.grammarNote: 'som' is the object of 'indgik' (entered into), referring back to 'aftale'; three coordinated subjects (staten, kommunen, universitetet) share that one verb.

sentences.text: Tanken er, at flere forskningsbaserede virksomheder skal opstå, når forskere og iværksættere får lettere adgang til hinanden.
sentences.translation: The idea is that more research-based companies should arise when researchers and entrepreneurs get easier access to each other.
sentences.grammarNote: 'når' introduces a repeated/future condition ('whenever this happens'), in contrast to 'da', which is reserved for a single completed event in the past.

sentences.text: Området har allerede tiltrukket investeringer og huser blandt andet et center for kunstig intelligens på universitetet.
sentences.translation: The area has already attracted investments and houses, among other things, a center for artificial intelligence at the university.
sentences.grammarNote: 'allerede' (already) sits right after 'har' â the standard position for sentence adverbs between the auxiliary and the past participle in present perfect.

sentences.text: Med den nye organisering håber aktørerne at kunne udvikle distriktet langsigtet og bæredygtigt.
sentences.translation: With the new organization, the stakeholders hope to be able to develop the district long-term and sustainably.
sentences.grammarNote: 'hÃ¥ber at kunne udvikle' â 'hÃ¥ber at' takes a bare infinitive (no separate subject), since the one hoping and the one developing are the same: 'aktÃ¸rerne'.

articles.title: Ny aftale skal gøre dansk forskning til flere virksomheder

sentences.text: Regeringen har indgået en bred politisk aftale, der skal gøre det lettere at omsætte dansk forskning til nye virksomheder.
sentences.translation: The government has made a broad political agreement that will make it easier to turn Danish research into new companies.
sentences.grammarNote: 'der' introduces a relative clause modifying 'aftale' (the agreement that...); 'gøre det lettere at + infinitive' is a fixed pattern for 'make it easier to'.

sentences.text: Aftalen betyder, at universiteterne fremover skal spille en mere central rolle som drivkraft for innovation og iværksætteri.
sentences.translation: The agreement means that universities will from now on play a more central role as a driving force for innovation and entrepreneurship.
sentences.grammarNote: 'fremover' (from now on) is a single-word time adverbial typical of formal/political Danish, placed right after the subject of the at-clause.

sentences.text: Målet er, at flere gode idéer fra forskningsverdenen hurtigere bliver til konkrete løsninger og højteknologiske virksomheder.
sentences.translation: The goal is for more good ideas from the research world to more quickly become concrete solutions and high-tech companies.
sentences.grammarNote: 'MÃ¥let er, at...' uses an at-clause as the subject complement â note the word order inside stays subject-verb, unlike German's verb-final pattern.

sentences.text: Uddannelses- og forskningsministeren siger, at Danmark skal være et af de bedste steder i verden at skabe innovation.
sentences.translation: The minister for education and research says that Denmark should be one of the best places in the world to create innovation.
sentences.grammarNote: 'et af de bedste steder i verden at + infinitive' is a fixed superlative-comparison chunk â worth learning as a whole phrase rather than building word by word.

sentences.text: Aftalen bygger på anbefalinger fra en taskforce, som i flere måneder har undersøgt, hvordan viden bedst kan overføres fra forskning til erhvervsliv.
sentences.translation: The agreement builds on recommendations from a task force, which for several months has examined how knowledge can best be transferred from research to business.
sentences.grammarNote: 'som' here is the all-purpose relative pronoun referring back to 'taskforce'; inside that clause, a second embedded question-word clause ('hvordan...') functions as the object of 'undersøgt'.

sentences.text: Erhvervsministeren kalder aftalen et svar på nogle af de udfordringer, som økonomen Mario Draghi har beskrevet i sin rapport om europæisk konkurrenceevne.
sentences.translation: The minister for business calls the agreement a response to some of the challenges that economist Mario Draghi has described in his report on European competitiveness.
sentences.grammarNote: 'som' here is the object of 'beskrevet' (described), referring back to 'udfordringer' â a nested relative clause modifying the noun phrase right before it.

sentences.text: Syv partier står bag aftalen, hvilket giver den et bredt flertal i Folketinget.
sentences.translation: Seven parties stand behind the agreement, which gives it a broad majority in Parliament.

words.grammar: refers to a whole preceding clause, not a single noun

sentences.grammarNote: 'hvilket' refers back to the entire preceding clause (the fact that seven parties support it), not to one specific noun â different from 'som/der', which always attach to a particular noun.

articles.title: Milliardaftale skal sikre stor energiø ved Bornholm

metadata.originalAuthor: Emma Sommer og Andreas Arp, Altinget

sentences.text: Danmark har taget endnu et skridt mod at bygge en kÃ¦mpe energiÃ¸ i ÃstersÃ¸en ud for Bornholm.
sentences.translation: Denmark has taken another step toward building a huge energy island in the Baltic Sea off Bornholm.

words.grammar: idiom: tage et skridt (take a step)
definite form used as the standard name

sentences.grammarNote: 'endnu et skridt' (yet another step) uses 'endnu' as an intensifier before 'et' â a different pattern from 'mere', and very common when describing gradual progress.

sentences.text: Regeringen og et bredt flertal af partier i Folketinget har indgået en aftale om at finansiere projektet med over 40 milliarder kroner.
sentences.translation: The government and a broad majority of parties in Parliament have made an agreement to finance the project with more than 40 billion kroner.
sentences.grammarNote: 'indgået en aftale om at + infinitive' is a fixed political construction for 'made an agreement to'; the present perfect marks this as a recently completed formal act.

sentences.text: Energiøen skal levere grøn strøm fra havvindmøller til både danske og tyske husstande.
sentences.translation: The energy island will deliver green electricity from offshore wind turbines to both Danish and German households.
sentences.grammarNote: 'skal levere' uses 'skal' to express a planned future function, not an obligation â a common B1 trap, since 'skal' usually maps to 'must' in English.

sentences.text: Projektet forventes at få en kapacitet på tre gigawatt, når det står færdigt.
sentences.translation: The project is expected to have a capacity of three gigawatts once it is finished.
sentences.grammarNote: 'når det står færdigt' uses 'når' for a future/repeated condition rather than 'da' (which is reserved for single past events); 'stå færdig' is an idiom meaning 'be finished'.

sentences.text: Klima-, energi- og forsyningsministeren siger, at den brede opbakning viser, at Danmark kan gennemføre store grønne projekter sammen på tværs af partier.
sentences.translation: The minister for climate, energy, and utilities says that the broad support shows that Danmark can carry out large green projects together across parties.
sentences.grammarNote: Two 'at'-clauses nested inside each other ('siger, at... viser, at...') â common in B1+ reported speech when one claim is used to support another.

sentences.text: Ifølge flere politikere styrker projektet også Europas energiuafhængighed i en tid med politisk usikkerhed.
sentences.translation: According to several politicians, the project also strengthens Europe's energy independence at a time of political uncertainty.

words.grammar: triggers V2 word order when sentence-initial

sentences.grammarNote: 'Ifølge flere politikere' is the first sentence element, so V2 still applies: the verb 'styrker' comes right after it, pushing the subject 'projektet' to third position.

sentences.text: Efter flere års forhandlinger er det nu lykkedes at finde finansieringen, så det første spadestik kan komme tættere på.
sentences.translation: After several years of negotiations, financing has now been found, so the first groundbreaking can come closer.
sentences.grammarNote: 'det lykkedes at + infinitive' is an impersonal construction meaning 'it succeeded in / they managed to' â useful for describing an achieved outcome without naming exactly who achieved it.

metadata.originalAuthor: Stine Haakonsson, lektor, Copenhagen Business School

sentences.text: Danmark har længe været førende inden for vindenergi og medicinalindustri, men nu er konkurrencen hårdere end nogensinde.
sentences.translation: Denmark has long been a leader in wind energy and the pharmaceutical industry, but now the competition is tougher than ever.

words.grammar: present participle used as adjective · from verb 'fÃ¸re'
definite form: konkurrence + -n suffix (not a separate article)

sentences.grammarNote: 'er hÃ¥rdere end nogensinde' uses the comparative + 'end nogensinde' (than ever) â a fixed B1 pattern for saying something has reached a new extreme.

sentences.text: Ifølge en lektor fra Copenhagen Business School rykker Kina hurtigt frem og tager flere af verdens vigtigste patenter.
sentences.translation: According to a lecturer from Copenhagen Business School, China is advancing quickly and taking more of the world's most important patents.

words.grammar: introduces reported claim · triggers V2 word order
Danish academic title, roughly between assistant and full professor
words.crossRef: German 'Lektor' (false-friend-adjacent: similar role, different ranking system)

words.grammar: rykke + frem · used idiomatically for competitive advancement

sentences.grammarNote: 'IfÃ¸lge X, [verb] + subject' â when a sentence opens with 'ifÃ¸lge' (according to), the verb still comes second (V2 rule), so the subject 'Kina' is pushed after the verb.

sentences.text: Samtidig er den globale orden blevet mere usikker, fordi handelskrige og politisk uro skaber problemer for forsyningskæderne.
sentences.translation: At the same time, the global order has become more uncertain, because trade wars and political unrest create problems for supply chains.
sentences.grammarNote: 'fordi' introduces a subordinate clause and sends the verb to the end of its own clause-internal position relative to the subject â note 'skaber' stays second within its clause, since 'fordi'-clauses keep normal word order in Danish (unlike German).

sentences.text: Danske virksomheder er afhængige af resten af verden, og derfor rammer disse forandringer Danmark hårdt.
sentences.translation: Danish companies are dependent on the rest of the world, and that's why these changes hit Denmark hard.

words.grammar: triggers V2: 'derfor rammer...' verb comes right after

sentences.grammarNote: 'afhÃ¦ngig af' always takes 'af' (of), not 'fra' â a fixed preposition pairing worth memorizing as a chunk.

sentences.text: Lektoren mener, at virksomheder, forskere og politikere skal samarbejde tættere for at beskytte Danmarks styrkepositioner.
sentences.translation: The lecturer believes that companies, researchers, and politicians need to work together more closely to protect Denmark's strong positions.
sentences.grammarNote: 'mener, at...' introduces an at-clause (like English 'that'); 'for at + infinitive' expresses purpose ('in order to').

sentences.text: Hun foreslår en national strategi, der kan hjælpe Danmark med at navigere i en verden, hvor alliancer og markeder hele tiden ændrer sig.
sentences.translation: She suggests a national strategy that can help Denmark navigate a world where alliances and markets are constantly changing.

words.grammar: Ã¦ndre + sig · reflexive when describing self-driven change

sentences.grammarNote: Two relative clauses chained together: 'der' refers back to 'strategi' (subject of its clause), 'hvor' refers back to 'verden' (a place/context). This kind of stacked relative clause is typical of B1-B2 written Danish.

sentences.text: Uden hurtig handling risikerer Danmark at miste sine førerpositioner inden for vigtige fremtidsteknologier.
sentences.translation: Without quick action, Denmark risks losing its leading positions in important future technologies.

words.grammar: used when possessor = subject of clause

sentences.grammarNote: 'risikere at + infinitiv' is a fixed construction for 'risk doing something'; 'sine' is the reflexive possessive, used because the thing owned (førerpositioner) belongs to the subject of the sentence (Danmark).

sentences.text: Efter et folketingsvalg i marts og over to måneders forhandlinger har Danmark fået en ny regering.
sentences.translation: After a parliamentary election in March and more than two months of negotiations, Denmark has gotten a new government.
sentences.grammarNote: 'Efter X og Y har Danmark fÃ¥et...' â the present perfect ('har fÃ¥et') links the outcome to the present moment, while 'efter' sets up the time frame with two coordinated noun phrases before the main clause even starts.

sentences.text: Statsminister Mette Frederiksen meddelte mandag aften, at hun kan danne regering sammen med SF, Moderaterne og Radikale Venstre.
sentences.translation: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen announced Monday evening that she can form a government together with SF, Moderaterne, and Radikale Venstre.
sentences.grammarNote: 'meddelte, at...' introduces reported speech as an at-clause â Danish news writing rarely uses direct quotation marks for this kind of factual announcement.

sentences.text: Forhandlingerne varede 71 dage og blev dermed de længste i Danmarks historie.
sentences.translation: The negotiations lasted 71 days and thereby became the longest in Denmark's history.
sentences.grammarNote: 'blev de længste' uses passive-form 'blev' + a superlative adjective ('længste' from 'lang'); 'dermed' (thereby) signals that this is a consequence of the fact just stated.

sentences.text: Valget i marts gav et meget uklart resultat, fordi 12 partier kom i Folketinget, og intet parti fik mere end 12 procent af stemmerne.
sentences.translation: The election in March gave a very unclear result, because 12 parties entered Parliament, and no party got more than 12 percent of the votes.

words.grammar: komme + i, idiomatic for entering a parliament/institution

sentences.grammarNote: 'fordi' introduces the cause and keeps normal subject-verb word order inside its own clause (unlike German, which sends the verb to the end); 'mere end' is the standard Danish comparative pattern, like English 'more than'.

sentences.text: Den nye regering har 21 ministre, og for første gang er der flere kvinder end mænd i en dansk regering.
sentences.translation: The new government has 21 ministers, and for the first time there are more women than men in a Danish government.
sentences.grammarNote: 'for første gang' is a fixed time adverbial; even placed first in its clause, the V2 rule still applies, so the verb 'er' comes immediately after it.

sentences.text: Kongen modtog den nye regering officielt på Amalienborg onsdag, hvor regeringen overtog magten fra den tidligere regering.
sentences.translation: The King officially received the new government at Amalienborg on Wednesday, where the government took over power from the previous government.
sentences.grammarNote: 'hvor' here introduces a relative clause referring back to a place ('Amalienborg'), the same pattern as English 'where' â different from 'der/som', which refer to people or things.

sentences.text: Det politiske indhold af regeringsgrundlaget â altsÃ¥ hvilken politik regeringen vil fÃ¸re â blev prÃ¦senteret kort efter pÃ¥ et pressemÃ¸de.
sentences.translation: The political content of the coalition agreement â that is, which policies the government will pursue â was presented shortly after at a press conference.
sentences.grammarNote: 'altsÃ¥' here works as a clarifying aside ('that is to say'), restating a formal term in simpler words â a common move in Danish journalism when introducing political vocabulary like 'regeringsgrundlag'.

sentences.text: Det er sommer i Danmark.
sentences.translation: It is summer in Denmark.

sentences.text: Solen skinner, og temperaturen er høj.
sentences.translation: The sun is shining, and the temperature is high.

words.grammar: høj · common gender; høj, høt, høje

sentences.text: Mange mennesker tager til stranden.
sentences.translation: Many people go to the beach.

sentences.text: Vejret er godt hele ugen.
sentences.translation: The weather is good all week.

sentences.text: Danmark er et skønt land om sommeren.
sentences.translation: Denmark is a beautiful country in the summer.

words.grammar: skøn · neuter: skønt · used here as predicate adj.

sentences.text: Et nyt museum åbner i København næste måned.
sentences.translation: A new museum opens in Copenhagen next month.

sentences.text: Museet fokuserer på dansk design og arkitektur.
sentences.translation: The museum focuses on Danish design and architecture.

words.grammar: fokusere (to focus) · present: fokuserer · borrowed from English

sentences.text: Der vil være udstillinger fra hele verden.
sentences.translation: There will be exhibitions from all over the world.

words.grammar: en udstilling · udstillinger (pl.) · from udstille (to exhibit)

sentences.text: Billetterne koster 150 kroner for voksne.
sentences.translation: The tickets cost 150 kroner for adults.

words.grammar: en billet · billetterne (def. pl.) · from French billet

sentences.text: Børn under 12 år kommer ind gratis.
sentences.translation: Children under 12 years old enter for free.

articles.title: Cykling er Danmarks sport nummer ét

sentences.text: Danskerne elsker at cykle.
sentences.translation: Danes love to cycle.

words.definition: to cycle, to ride a bike

sentences.text: Over halvdelen af alle danskere cykler til arbejde.
sentences.translation: Over half of all Danes cycle to work.

words.grammar: en halvdel · halvdelen (def.) · halvdel = half part

sentences.text: København er en af verdens mest cykelfravenlige byer.
sentences.translation: Copenhagen is one of the world's most bike-friendly cities.

sentences.text: Der er mange cykelstier i hele landet.
sentences.translation: There are many bike paths throughout the country.

sentences.text: Cyklingen er god for miljøet og sundheden.
sentences.translation: Cycling is good for the environment and health.

words.grammar: et miljø · miljøet (def.) · from French milieu

sentences.text: En ny undersøgelse viser, at danskerne spiser sundere end tidligere.
sentences.translation: A new study shows that Danes eat healthier than before.

sentences.text: Forbruget af grøntsager er steget med 20 procent på fem år.
sentences.translation: The consumption of vegetables has increased by 20 percent over five years.

words.grammar: et forbrug · forbruget (def.) · for- + bruge (to use)

sentences.text: Flere danskere vælger plantebaserede måltider.
sentences.translation: More Danes are choosing plant-based meals.

words.grammar: vælge (to choose) · present: vælger · past: valgte

sentences.text: Eksperter mener, at denne tendens er positiv for folkesundheden.
sentences.translation: Experts believe that this trend is positive for public health.

words.grammar: en tendens · tendensen (def.) · from Latin tendere

sentences.text: Regeringen vil støtte lokale landmænd, der producerer sunde fødevarer.
sentences.translation: The government will support local farmers who produce healthy food.

words.grammar: en landmand · landmænd (pl.) · irregular plural with umlaut

articles.title: Dansk film vinder pris i Cannes

sentences.text: En dansk film har vundet en prestigefyldt pris på filmfestivalen i Cannes.
sentences.translation: A Danish film has won a prestigious award at the film festival in Cannes.

words.grammar: vinde (to win) · past participle: vundet · irregular

sentences.text: Filmen handler om en ung kvinde, der rejser rundt i Skandinavien.
sentences.translation: The film is about a young woman who travels around Scandinavia.

words.grammar: handle om (to be about) · handle (inf.) · handler (present)

sentences.text: Instruktøren siger, at filmen er inspireret af hans barndom i Jylland.
sentences.translation: The director says that the film is inspired by his childhood in Jutland.

words.grammar: en instruktør · instruktøren (def.) · from French instructeur

sentences.text: Mange danskere er stolte over succesen.
sentences.translation: Many Danes are proud of the success.

words.grammar: en succes · succesen (def.) · from French succès

sentences.text: Filmen vises i danske biografer fra næste fredag.
sentences.translation: The film will be shown in Danish cinemas from next Friday.

words.grammar: vise (to show) · passive: vises · -s passive construction
en biograf · biografer (pl.) · from Greek biographos
